Python Basics.question: What is a list?
Python Basics.answer: An ordered, mutable collection of items

Python Basics.question: What is a dictionary?
Python Basics.answer: A collection of key-value pairs

Python Basics.question: What does len() do?
Python Basics.answer: Returns the number of items in an object

Python Basics.question: What is a list comprehension?
Python Basics.answer: A concise way to create lists: [x for x in iterable]

Python Basics.question: What is the difference between append() and extend()?
Python Basics.answer: append() adds a single item, extend() adds all items from an iterable

Python Basics.question: What is a lambda function?
Python Basics.answer: An anonymous one-line function defined with the lambda keyword

Python Basics.question: What is the difference between == and is?
Python Basics.answer: == checks value equality, is checks if two variables point to the same object

Python Basics.question: What is a tuple?
Python Basics.answer: An ordered, immutable collection of items

Python Basics.question: What does the pass keyword do?
Python Basics.answer: Acts as a placeholder that does nothing

Python Basics.question: What is a set?
Python Basics.answer: An unordered collection of unique items

Python Basics.question: What is the difference between a list and a tuple?
Python Basics.answer: Lists are mutable, tuples are immutable

Python Basics.question: What does the zip() function do?
Python Basics.answer: Combines multiple iterables into tuples element by element

Python Basics.question: What is a decorator?
Python Basics.answer: A function that wraps another function to modify its behavior

Python Basics.question: What is the difference between *args and **kwargs?
Python Basics.answer: *args passes variable positional arguments, **kwargs passes variable keyword arguments

Python Basics.question: What is a generator?
Python Basics.answer: A function that yields values one at a time using the yield keyword instead of return

Python Basics.question: What does enumerate() do?
Python Basics.answer: Returns an iterator of tuples containing the index and value of each item

Python Basics.question: What is the difference between deep copy and shallow copy?
Python Basics.answer: Shallow copy copies references, deep copy copies the actual objects recursively

Python Basics.question: What is None in Python?
Python Basics.answer: A special constant representing the absence of a value

Python Basics.question: What does the map() function do?
Python Basics.answer: Applies a function to every item in an iterable and returns a map object

Python Basics.question: What is exception handling?
Python Basics.answer: Using try/except blocks to catch and handle errors gracefully

Python Basics.question: What is the global keyword?
Python Basics.answer: Declares that a variable inside a function refers to a globally scoped variable

Python Basics.question: What is a module in Python?
Python Basics.answer: A file containing Python code that can be imported and used in other files

Python Basics.question: What does the filter() function do?
Python Basics.answer: Filters items from an iterable based on a function that returns True or False

Python Basics.question: What is the difference between break and continue?
Python Basics.answer: break exits the loop entirely, continue skips to the next iteration

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is Big-O notation?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A way to describe time or space complexity as input size grows

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is the time complexity of binary search?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: O(log n)

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is a linked list?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A linear data structure where each element points to the next via a pointer

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is a stack?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A data structure that follows Last In First Out (LIFO) order

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is a queue?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A data structure that follows First In First Out (FIFO) order

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is the time complexity of accessing an array element?
What is a hash table?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A data structure that maps keys to values using a hash function for O(1) average lookups

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is a binary tree?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A tree where each node has at most two children

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is the difference between BFS and DFS?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: BFS explores level by level using a queue, DFS explores depth-first using a stack or recursion

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is the time complexity of quicksort on average?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: O(n log n)

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is a heap?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A tree-based data structure where the parent is always greater than or less than its children

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is dynamic programming?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: Breaking a problem into subproblems and storing results to avoid redundant computation

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is memoization?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: Caching the results of expensive function calls to avoid recomputation

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is the time complexity of inserting into a hash table?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: O(1) on average

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is a graph?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A data structure of nodes connected by edges, can be directed or undirected

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is the difference between a tree and a graph?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A tree is a graph with no cycles and a single root node

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is bubble sort?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A sorting algorithm that repeatedly swaps adjacent elements if they are in the wrong order, O(n^2)

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is merge sort?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A divide and conquer sorting algorithm with O(n log n) time complexity

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is a trie?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A tree data structure used to store strings where each node represents a character

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is a two pointer technique?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: Using two pointers to traverse an array simultaneously to solve problems efficiently

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is a sliding window?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A technique that maintains a subset of data within a window that slides across an array

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is recursion?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A function that calls itself until a base case is reached

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is the space complexity of a recursive function?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: O(n) due to the call stack growing with each recursive call

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is an adjacency list?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: A way to represent a graph where each node stores a list of its neighbors

Data Structures & Algorithms.question: What is the difference between a min heap and a max heap?
Data Structures & Algorithms.answer: Min heap: smallest element at root. Max heap: largest element at root

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is encapsulation?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: Bundling data and methods within a class and restricting direct access from outside

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is inheritance?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A mechanism where a class derives properties and methods from a parent class

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is polymorphism?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: The ability of different objects to respond to the same method in different ways

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is abstraction?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: Hiding complex implementation details and exposing only necessary features

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is a constructor?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A special method called when an object is instantiated to initialize its attributes

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is the difference between a class and an object?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A class is a blueprint, an object is an instance of that class

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is method overriding?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: Redefining a parent class method in a child class to change its behavior

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is method overloading?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: Defining multiple methods with the same name but different parameters

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is an interface?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A contract that defines methods a class must implement without providing implementation

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is an abstract class?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A class that cannot be instantiated and may contain abstract methods subclasses must implement

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is the difference between public, private, and protected?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: Public: accessible anywhere. Private: accessible only within the class. Protected: accessible within class and subclasses

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is a static method?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A method that belongs to the class rather than an instance and cannot access instance variables

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is composition?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: Building complex objects by combining simpler objects rather than using inheritance

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is the difference between composition and inheritance?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: Inheritance is an is-a relationship, composition is a has-a relationship

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is a destructor?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A method called when an object is destroyed to release resources

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is coupling?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: The degree of dependency between classes, lower coupling is better for maintainability

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is cohesion?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: How closely related the responsibilities of a class are, higher cohesion is better

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is the Single Responsibility Principle?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A class should have only one reason to change, meaning it should have only one job

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is the Open/Closed Principle?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: Classes should be open for extension but closed for modification

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is a design pattern?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A reusable solution to a commonly occurring problem in software design

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is the Singleton pattern?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A design pattern that ensures only one instance of a class exists

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is the Factory pattern?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A design pattern that creates objects without specifying the exact class to instantiate

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is the Observer pattern?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: A design pattern where objects subscribe to events and get notified when they occur

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is duck typing?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: If an object has the required methods and properties, it can be used regardless of its type

Object Oriented Programming.question: What is the difference between an instance variable and a class variable?
Object Oriented Programming.answer: Instance variables are unique to each object, class variables are shared across all instances

Web Development.question: What is HTTP?
Web Development.answer: HyperText Transfer Protocol, the foundation of data communication on the web

Web Development.question: What is the difference between GET and POST?
Web Development.answer: GET retrieves data, POST sends data to the server to create or update a resource

Web Development.question: What is a REST API?
Web Development.answer: An API that uses HTTP methods and follows stateless communication principles

Web Development.question: What is JSON?
Web Development.answer: JavaScript Object Notation, a lightweight format for storing and transporting data

Web Development.question: What is the DOM?
Web Development.answer: Document Object Model, a tree representation of HTML elements that JavaScript can manipulate

Web Development.question: What is the difference between HTML, CSS, and JavaScript?
Web Development.answer: HTML is structure, CSS is styling, JavaScript is behavior

Web Development.question: What is a 404 error?
Web Development.answer: A HTTP status code meaning the requested resource was not found

Web Development.question: What is a 500 error?
Web Development.answer: A HTTP status code meaning an internal server error occurred

Web Development.question: What is CORS?
Web Development.answer: Cross Origin Resource Sharing, a security feature that controls which domains can access an API

Web Development.question: What is a cookie?
Web Development.answer: A small piece of data stored in the browser used to remember information between sessions

Web Development.question: What is a session?
Web Development.answer: Server-side storage of user data that persists across multiple requests

Web Development.question: What is the difference between authentication and authorization?
Web Development.answer: Authentication verifies who you are, authorization determines what you can access

Web Development.question: What is JWT?
Web Development.answer: JSON Web Token, a compact way to securely transmit information between parties as a JSON object

Web Development.question: What is a CDN?
Web Development.answer: Content Delivery Network, a distributed network of servers that delivers content faster based on location

Web Development.question: What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL?
Web Development.answer: SQL uses structured tables with schemas, NoSQL uses flexible document or key-value storage

Web Development.question: What is an ORM?
Web Development.answer: Object Relational Mapper, a tool that lets you interact with a database using objects instead of SQL

Web Development.question: What is caching?
Web Development.answer: Storing frequently accessed data temporarily to reduce load times and server requests

Web Development.question: What is a webhook?
Web Development.answer: A way for an app to send real-time data to another app when an event occurs

Web Development.question: What is the difference between localStorage and sessionStorage?
Web Development.answer: localStorage persists after the browser closes, sessionStorage is cleared when the tab closes

Web Development.question: What is a load balancer?
Web Development.answer: A server that distributes incoming traffic across multiple servers to prevent overload

Web Development.question: What is responsive design?
Web Development.answer: Designing web pages that adapt their layout to different screen sizes

Web Development.question: What is a microservice?
Web Development.answer: An architectural style where an app is built as small independent services that communicate via APIs

Web Development.question: What is CI/CD?
Web Development.answer: Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment, automating the process of testing and deploying code

Web Development.question: What is Docker?
Web Development.answer: A platform that packages applications into containers so they run consistently across environments

Web Development.question: What is an index in a database?
Web Development.answer: A data structure that speeds up data retrieval by allowing faster lookups on specific columns

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is supervised learning?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: Training a model on labeled data where the correct output is already known

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is unsupervised learning?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: Training a model on unlabeled data to find hidden patterns or groupings

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is a neural network?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A model inspired by the brain consisting of layers of interconnected nodes that learn from data

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is overfitting?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: When a model performs well on training data but poorly on new unseen data

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is underfitting?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: When a model is too simple to capture the underlying patterns in the data

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is a training set vs test set?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: Training set is used to train the model, test set is used to evaluate its performance on unseen data

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is gradient descent?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: An optimization algorithm that minimizes a loss function by iteratively adjusting model parameters

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is a loss function?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A function that measures how far a model's predictions are from the actual values

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is a hyperparameter?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A parameter set before training that controls the learning process, such as learning rate or batch size

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is transfer learning?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: Reusing a pre-trained model on a new but related task to save time and data

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is a convolutional neural network?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A neural network designed for image processing that uses convolutional layers to detect features

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is a large language model?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A neural network trained on massive amounts of text data to understand and generate language

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is reinforcement learning?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: Training an agent to make decisions by rewarding desired behaviors and penalizing undesired ones

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is a confusion matrix?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A table that summarizes the performance of a classification model showing true/false positives and negatives

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is precision?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: The proportion of positive predictions that were actually correct

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is recall?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: The proportion of actual positives that were correctly identified by the model

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is feature engineering?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: The process of selecting and transforming raw data into features that improve model performance

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is normalization?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: Scaling input data to a standard range, typically 0 to 1, to improve model training

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is a decision tree?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A model that splits data into branches based on feature values to make predictions

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is random forest?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: An ensemble of decision trees that combines their predictions to improve accuracy

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is k-means clustering?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: An unsupervised algorithm that groups data into k clusters based on similarity

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is a transformer model?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A neural network architecture that uses attention mechanisms, foundational to modern LLMs

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is attention in machine learning?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A mechanism that allows a model to focus on the most relevant parts of the input when making predictions

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is cross-validation?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A technique that evaluates a model by training and testing on different subsets of data multiple times

AI & Machine Learning.question: What is regularization?
AI & Machine Learning.answer: A technique that adds a penalty to the loss function to reduce overfitting by discouraging complex models
